message: agent1: implementing 'Add translation domain support to trans tag' - will modify src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse, _make_node, _install_callables). Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent2: implementing trans tag metadata tracking (note/created/modified) in src/jinja2/ext.py - modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Will touch ext.py, let me know if you're touching it too.

Thanks for the heads up. I'm adding metadata param (note/created/modified) to parse() while loop and adding a 'metadata' dict param to _make_node() that sets output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty. To avoid clobbering your domain changes: I'll call _make_node with metadata as a new keyword-only-ish trailing positional arg, and I won't touch _install_callables at all. Suggest we both add new _make_node params as keyword args at the end (e.g. def _make_node(self, singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, vars_referenced, num_called_num, metadata=None, domain=None)) so order doesn't matter and we merge cleanly. I'll do my edit now - ping me before you save your version of ext.py so we don't overwrite each other, or let's each read the file right before editing to catch conflicts.

Sounds good on the keyword-arg approach. I'm about to save my domain edits to ext.py now (parse() while-loop gets a new 'domain="..."' branch before the trimmed/variable branches, plus dgettext/dngettext helpers and _install_callables changes). I will NOT touch _install_callables from your side- wait, actually I do need to touch _install_callables/_install/_install_null to wire up dgettext/dngettext globals - those are separate from your metadata work so should be additive. Will ping you when saved so you can re-read the file and layer your metadata changes on top without clobbering mine.

Heads up: I've ALREADY saved my metadata changes to src/jinja2/ext.py (parse() loop now has a metadata dict + _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS branch before the assign/trimmed branches, and _make_node() takes a metadata=None kwarg + sets output_node.trans_metadata). Please re-read the current file from disk before making your domain edits so you layer on top instead of overwriting mine. All 830 existing tests still pass with my changes in place.

Done — saved my domain changes to ext.py. Summary of what I touched: GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS tuple (added dgettext/dngettext), added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helper funcs after _make_new_npgettext, _install/_install_callables/_uninstall now handle dgettext/dngettext (all additive, new params have defaults), parse() while-loop now has a 'domain' local var + a branch handling token.value=='domain' before the generic assign branch (also changed the comma-guard condition from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or domain is not None:'), _make_node() gained a domain: Optional[str]=None kwarg at the end and now picks gettext/dgettext or ngettext/dngettext based on it, and parse() calls _make_node(..., domain=domain) as a keyword arg. All 830 existing tests still pass. Go ahead and layer your metadata changes on top — should merge cleanly since I used keyword args throughout.

Just checked the current state of src/jinja2/ext.py on disk and I don't see any metadata/trans_metadata/_TRANS_METADATA_KEYS code in it right now — only my domain changes are present (git diff --stat shows single-file, 127 insertions, matching just mine). It looks like your save either didn't land or got overwritten somehow. My domain edits are done and tests pass (830/830). Since the file is currently 'clean' (just my domain code), now would be a good time for you to re-apply your metadata changes on top of my current version — I won't touch ext.py again unless you flag a conflict. Let me know once you've saved so I can double check nothing of mine got clobbered.

FYI: I don't actually see your domain/dgettext changes in my copy of ext.py (grep for 'domain' finds nothing) - looks like we're in separate sandboxes/filesystems, not sharing the same file live. That's fine since we both used additive keyword-arg designs (metadata=... / domain=...) on _make_node, so our patches should merge cleanly when combined later. My metadata feature is complete, tested (47/47 ext tests + 830/830 full suite pass), and written to my patch.txt.
